title: Made to Stick: Why Some Ideas Survive and Others Die
subtitle: A field-tested framework for understanding why certain ideas take root in our minds while dozens of others — equally smart, equally important — fizzle and are forgotten.
author: Chip Heath & Dan Heath
excerpt: In Made to Stick, Chip and Dan Heath answer a deceptively simple question: why do so many great ideas die in obscurity while half-baked urban legends spread across the globe overnight? The answer, they argue, comes down to six hallmarks — Simple, Unexpected, Concrete, Credible, Emotional, and Stories — together a mnemonic called SUCCES. Drawing on psychology research, organisational case studies, and counter-intuitive examples from urban legends to Southwest Airlines, the book provides a practical blueprint for making any idea memorable, actionable, and long-lasting. It is, in the authors' own terms, a sticky book about making ideas sticky.
